name: What causes hollow spots or squeaks after flooring is installed?

mainEntity.name: Why does my new floor sound hollow or squeak after installation?
mainEntity.text: Why does my new floor sound hollow or squeak after installation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Hollow sounds and squeaks after installation usually point to a problem under the flooring rather than with the top surface alone. Common causes include loose subfloor panels, uneven framing, insufficient fasteners, gaps between underlayment and subfloor, or movement in the structure below. Floating floors can also sound hollow if the underlayment is not correct or the subfloor is not flat. The solution depends on the cause. Sometimes the floor can be corrected by tightening the subfloor, filling low spots, or improving underlayment support. In other cases, sections may need to be lifted and reinstalled. If the noise is coming from a structural issue, cosmetic fixes will not solve it. A proper installation should start with subfloor inspection, fastening, and leveling before any finish flooring goes down. If your new floor is already making noise, document the problem early and ask the installer to inspect it before the issue spreads or becomes harder to repair.
acceptedAnswer.description: Hollow sounds or squeaks usually come from an uneven subfloor, loose fasteners, poor underlayment contact, or movement in the structure below. These issues are often caused by missing prep, not the flooring material itself.
